title: lagopus arvensis
intro: Lagopus arvensis, also known as the marsh marigold, is a perennial flowering plant native to wetlands in Europe and Asia. The plant's roots and flowers have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, as they contain alkaloids that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Lagopus arvensis have been used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are often used topically or taken orally to harness its health benefits.
